inclusion_criteria: Human postmortem brain studies including individuals with schizophrenia diagnosed by DSM/ICD or documented clinical assessment
Include a healthy, non‑schizophrenic postmortem control group

Report quantitative group-level data for at least one neuroinflammatory outcome: microglial markers (e.g., Iba1, CD68, HLA‑DR), GFAP expression, glial cell densities, cytokines, arachidonic cascade markers (e.g., COX‑2, cPLA2), substance P, SERPINA3, or IFITM
Original, peer‑reviewed research with sufficient information to extract or compute group comparisons
exclusion_criteria: Non-human studies or non-postmortem brain studies (e.g., animal/cell models, peripheral tissue, CSF/serum only, in vivo neuroimaging)
No healthy control group, schizophrenia not primary diagnosis/unclear diagnostic criteria, or cohorts with major neurodegenerative/inflammatory CNS comorbidities that confound neuroinflammation
Do not measure any listed neuroinflammatory markers or lack extractable group-level data for cases and controls
Non-original publications (reviews, editorials, meta-analyses) or duplicate reporting of the same postmortem cases
screening_criteria.description: Screen original human postmortem brain studies comparing schizophrenia cases to healthy controls that report quantitative neuroinflammatory markers (microglia-related markers, GFAP, glial densities, cytokines, arachidonic cascade markers, substance P, SERPINA3, IFITM).

included_articles.title: Glial fibrillary acidic protein mRNA levels in the cingulate cortex of individuals with depression, bipolar disorder and schizophrenia.

Increase in HLA-DR immunoreactive microglia in frontal and temporal cortex of chronic schizophrenics.
Immunohistochemical localization of phosphorylated glial fibrillary acidic protein in the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus from patients with schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and depression.
Regionally specific changes in levels of cortical S100beta in bipolar 1 disorder but not schizophrenia.
Increased expression of astrocyte markers in schizophrenia: Association with neuroinflammation.
Fibrillary astrocytes are decreased in the subgenual cingulate in schizophrenia.
Inflammation-related genes up-regulated in schizophrenia brains.
Evidence for morphological alterations in prefrontal white matter glia in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder.
3-D Golgi and image analysis of the olfactory tubercle in schizophrenia.
Extracellular matrix-glial abnormalities in the amygdala and entorhinal cortex of subjects diagnosed with schizophrenia.
Calprotectin in microglia from frontal cortex is up-regulated in schizophrenia: evidence for an inflammatory process?
Molecular mechanisms and timing of cortical immune activation in schizophrenia.
Elevated viral restriction factor levels in cortical blood vessels in schizophrenia.
